task_completion.description: Verify that a clinical evaluation or encounter is opened for the correct patient.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the GNU Health interface showing a patient record or active clinical encounter for 'John Zenon'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the occupational exposure diagnosis is documented.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the diagnosis or conditions section within the encounter showing the ICD-10 code 'T75.2' or 'I73.0' (or their text descriptions like 'Effects of vibration' or 'Raynaud's syndrome').

task_completion.description: Verify that a vasodilator is prescribed to manage vasospastic symptoms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a medication or prescription screen showing an order for either 'Nifedipine' or 'Amlodipine'.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least two laboratory tests are ordered to rule out secondary causes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a lab test request or orders screen showing at least two distinct tests requested (e.g., ANA, CRP, ESR, CBC).

task_completion.description: Verify that a surveillance follow-up appointment is scheduled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an appointment scheduling screen or confirmation showing a future appointment date set for John Zenon. The date should be visibly 21 to 45 days ahead of the system's current date.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GNU Health graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the GNU Health desktop client GUI being actively used, rather than terminal commands or database scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a text editor or mock application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface must be the actual GNU Health application with its standard menus, tabs, and icons, not a notepad or generic text editor.

integrity.description: Verify that data was entered into the appropriate structured fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: Diagnoses, prescriptions, and lab orders should be entered in their respective dedicated modules/tabs, not just typed as a single block of text into a general notes field.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Patient: John Zenon. Required Diagnosis Codes: T75.2 or I73.0. Required Medication: Nifedipine or Amlodipine. Required Lab Orders: At least 2 new tests (e.g., ANA, CRP, ESR, CBC). Required Follow-up: An appointment scheduled exactly 21 to 45 days from the current system date.
